title: codon_stats output row
description: One TSV row per input pair. NaN values appear as 'NA'; positive infinity as 'Inf' (when JC saturation hits p >= 0.75).

n_aligned_bp.description: Length of each aligned sequence (assumed equal).

n_codons_total.description: Total codons in the alignment (n_aligned_bp / 3, integer division).

n_codons_used.description: Codons contributing to NG86 statistics: both sequences ACGT, neither codon a stop.

n_codons_gap.description: Codons skipped because either sequence had a non-ACGT character ('-', 'N', etc.).

n_codons_stop.description: Codons skipped because at least one sequence had a stop codon (TAA, TAG, TGA).

n_4d_sites.description: Codons where position 3 is 4-fold degenerate in BOTH sequences AND positions 1–2 match between the two sequences. These count toward d4d (neutral rate proxy).

n_4d_diffs.description: Subset of n_4d_sites where position 3 differs between the two sequences.

S.description: Synonymous-site count (NG86), summed across used codons. Average of S_per_codon over the two sequences.

sd.description: Synonymous substitution count, path-averaged over codon differences (1 path for nd=1, 2 for nd=2, 6 for nd=3, stop-containing paths discarded).

pS.description: Proportion of synonymous differences = sd/S. NA if S == 0.

pN.description: Proportion of nonsynonymous differences = nd/N. NA if N == 0.

dS.description: Jukes-Cantor corrected synonymous divergence (Ks). 'Inf' if pS >= 0.75 (JC saturated); NA if pS is NA.

dN.description: Jukes-Cantor corrected nonsynonymous divergence (Ka). 'Inf' if pN >= 0.75.

omega.description: dN/dS ratio. NA if dS is NA, Inf, or 0.

p4d.description: Raw proportion of differences at 4-fold degenerate sites = n_4d_diffs / n_4d_sites. NA if n_4d_sites == 0.

d4d.description: JC-corrected 4D divergence — most-neutral substitution-rate proxy. 'Inf' if p4d >= 0.75.

NG86.site_counting: Per-codon S = sum over 3 positions of (#synonymous 1-step neighbours / 3). Pair S = average of the two codons' S values.
NG86.substitution_counting: Path-averaged; stop-containing intermediates dropped; nd=1 ⇒ 1 path, nd=2 ⇒ 2 paths, nd=3 ⇒ 6 paths.
NG86.distance_correction: Jukes-Cantor 1969: d = -3/4 * ln(1 - 4/3 p). Saturation at p >= 0.75 ⇒ Inf.
NG86.fourfold_degenerate: Position 3 is 4D iff all 4 codons in the same first-two-nt family (Leu CTN, Val GTN, Pro CCN, Thr ACN, Ala GCN, Gly GGN, Arg CGN, Ser TCN) encode the same amino acid. A site contributes to d4d only if BOTH codons have a 4D 3rd position AND positions 1–2 match between the two sequences.

YN00.status: planned (Turn 2 of this session): Yang & Nielsen 2000 κ-corrected NG with transition/transversion bias estimated iteratively from the data.
